syntheticReason: An ODS API instance was not available when DMS-1145 was implemented. These ODS-shape fixtures are hand-authored to match the wire shape an ODS API would emit (link.rel + link.href present on each fully-defined document reference, GUIDs rendered in N format). OdsParityContractTests covers two comparison axes: (1) It_emits_link_with_parity_to_ods asserts the fixture has the expected ODS-shape values, and (2) It_emits_link_byte_equal_to_ods_fixture_via_dms_reconstitution drives DMS reconstitution end-to-end on semantically equivalent input and asserts DMS's emitted link.rel/link.href match the fixture's link.rel/link.href (with GUID-format normalization). These fixtures stand as expected-wire-shape regression coverage; actual DMS link emission is covered by DocumentReconstituterLinkEmissionTests and the 29a-29e relational integration tests. If recorded ODS evidence later becomes necessary for migration validation, swap in real responses and flip synthetic=false plus the version/endpoint/date fields; both test axes continue to provide value in that scenario.
guidFormatNormalization: The ODS API renders document UUIDs in 32-char N format (no hyphens), e.g. 'abcd1234ef0156782a3b4c5d6e7f8001'. DMS renders in 36-char D format with hyphens, e.g. 'abcd1234-ef01-5678-2a3b-4c5d6e7f8001'. The parity test parses both forms via System.Guid.Parse and compares the canonical D-format string. ODS and DMS reference the same logical document UUID; only the rendering differs.
scope: Document references only (descriptor references keep their canonical-URI string surface — see link-injection.md). V1 parity covers one concrete reference shape (AcademicWeek -> School) and one abstract reference shape (Course.educationOrganizationReference resolving to the concrete School subclass). Full resource coverage is a follow-on.
sunsetTrigger: Delete this entire folder (fixture-metadata.json, both *.ods.json fixtures, OdsParityContractTests.cs, and the corresponding <None Update="LinkInjection\OdsParity\*.json"> block in EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj) once ODS is deprecated as a reference implementation and the ODS-to-DMS migration window has closed. Rationale: parity-with-ODS is only meaningful while both systems coexist as wire contracts. Post-deprecation, DMS IS the wire contract — the rel/href shape is already enforced by DocumentReconstituterLinkEmissionTests (unit) and the 29a-29e integration tests, so no coverage is lost. The harness types in Backend.Tests.Common (ReferenceLocator, LinkInjectionAssertions, DeterministicLinkSlugResolver) STAY — they are used by the live integration tests independently of ODS parity.
